key_points: Time is measured using standard units such as seconds, minutes and hours
Clocks and watches are used to measure time
Motion refers to the movement of an object from one place to another
Distance tells how far an object has moved
Speed tells how fast an object moves
examples: A car travelling 60 km in 1 hour has a speed of 60 km/h
A runner moving around a track shows motion
exam_tips: Remember the relationship between speed, distance and time
Always write the correct units such as km, m, hours or seconds
Read the question carefully to identify what needs to be calculated
